title: Audit whether MLB model confidence is calibrated, inverted, noisy, or structurally invalid
objective: Determine whether the current confidence score is calibrated at all, inverted, noisy, or structurally disconnected from real outcomes, and define how it should be measured against realized results.
paper_only_note: This is paper-only / observation-mode work. No model implementation or live strategy change is authorized from this task.
required_work: 1. Measure confidence buckets against realized outcomes.
2. Test for inversion, monotonicity failure, and noise.
3. Separate contamination/outlier effects from genuine calibration failure where possible.
4. Recommend whether confidence should be rescaled, retrained, replaced, or redefined, but do not implement.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit note on contamination vs structural failure
verification: State whether confidence is calibrated, inverted, noisy, or invalid
Quote bucket evidence and any caveats
